steps.title: Identify the right triangle and sides
steps.content:
The small square at the bottom right indicates a right angle. The triangle has:
  • Hypotenuse (longest side, opposite the right angle) = 21
  • One leg (vertical side) = 6
  • Other leg (horizontal side) = $a$ (what we're solving for)

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem and solve for a
steps.content:
For a right triangle, the Pythagorean theorem states:
$$a^2 + b^2 = c^2$$

Substitute the known values:
$$a^2 + 6^2 = 21^2$$

Isolate $a^2$:
$$a^2 = 21^2 - 6^2$$

Take the square root of both sides:
$$a = \sqrt{21^2 - 6^2}$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
